area: Runner build telemetry / incremental benchmark / decision quality
finding: The sealed runner selected `incrementalBuild`, but the observed runtime sample was slower than recent `fullRebuild` samples. Without matched A/B grouping, operators can misread an incremental decision as an actual speedup even when fixed costs dominate.
expectedBehavior: Runner telemetry should provide a matched comparison envelope or explicitly mark unpaired speedup claims as inconclusive. Build guidance should distinguish `decision=incrementalBuild` from `outcome=faster`, and expose phase-level dominant-cost hints before reporting acceleration percentages.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0196 closeout, `.atm/runtime/telemetry/runner-sync-build/2026-07-20T04-27-56-146Z-runner-sync.jsonl` recorded `buildDecision=incrementalBuild`, `changedPathCount=3`, `affectedPackageCount=2`, and `totalElapsed=42741ms`. Recent full rebuild runtime telemetry in the same directory included samples around `24116ms` and `24167ms`, making this unpaired incremental sample roughly 77% slower than the nearest full sample. Dominant phases included worktree setup, TypeScript build, root-drop assembly, and artifact sync.
followUp: Have ATM-GOV-0201 or ATM-GOV-0205 define a canonical paired build timing interface with matched full/incremental/cache samples, speedup confidence labels, and phase-attribution fields consumed by build guidance.
